decisions.title: Commit to Explore V2 (Channel List v2) GA by Q2 2026
decisions.context: ENG-9115 and ENG-9116 show active development on channel list v2 with pinning and drag-to-plot features. This is core to 'Nail the Core' pillar. Need to decide if we're shipping this as the default experience or continuing parallel development.

arguments.for: Signal indicates momentum. Channel list is the most-used UI element. Improving it directly increases Decisions Captured Per Customer by reducing friction in data exploration.
arguments.against: Multiple UX polish tickets (ENG-9106, ENG-9017, ENG-9001) suggest foundational issues. Shipping prematurely could hurt adoption and require rollback.

decisions.title: Prioritize downsampling performance vs new Python Rules features
decisions.context: ENG-8790, ENG-9040, ENG-9041 are all downsampling improvements. Meanwhile ENG-9146, ENG-9147 are Python Rules infrastructure. Both compete for eng resources. Downsampling affects Explore performance (Nail the Core), Python Rules is Build the Moat.

arguments.for: Downsampling directly impacts every user's daily experience. Poor chart performance is #1 complaint in NPS. ENG-8866 shows real user pain with data flash errors.
arguments.against: Python Rules is strategic differentiation and 'Build the Moat'. K2 Mission pipeline (ENG-9104) may require rules capability. Delaying could lose competitive edge.

decisions.title: Allocate dedicated team to Keycloak/auth stability
decisions.context: ENG-7813, ENG-8852, ENG-8293 show ongoing auth issues including govcloud migration, registration failures, and redirect loops. These block 'Embed Deeper' for enterprise customers with strict auth requirements.
decisions.blockedBy: Govcloud migration dependencies unclear, no dedicated auth eng resource identified

arguments.for: Auth issues prevent users from accessing the product entirely. ENG-8293 redirect loop is a showstopper. Enterprise deals require govcloud. This is table stakes.
arguments.against: Auth is infrastructure work that doesn't directly increase Decisions Captured. Team is already stretched on Explore V2 and Rules work.

decisions.title: Ship data selector polish (ENG-9017) before Channel List v2 GA
decisions.context: Data selector is the entry point to building charts. If polish issues exist, they'll be amplified when Channel List v2 drives more usage. Need sequencing decision.

arguments.for: Better to fix foundation before scaling usage. Data selector polish is smaller scope than full Channel List v2 and can ship faster.
arguments.against: Delays Channel List v2 momentum. Teams can work in parallel if scoped correctly. Data selector polish may uncover more issues.

decisions.title: Establish product telemetry standard using fixed Amplitude implementation
decisions.context: ENG-8158, ENG-9088, ENG-9145 show Amplitude causing CPU issues, numeric email bugs, requiring redeploy. We can't measure Decisions Captured Per Customer without reliable product analytics.
decisions.owner: Head of Product + Head of Engineering

arguments.for: North Star metric depends on measurement. Current state means we're flying blind on actual usage patterns. Need this to validate strategy.
arguments.against: Amplitude may not be fixable; might need to evaluate alternatives (Mixpanel, PostHog). That's a bigger project that delays getting clean data.

northStar.name: Decisions Captured Per Customer
northStar.delta: N/A - measurement blocked by ENG-8158, ENG-9088
